title: convolvulus cneorum subsp. latifolius
intro: Convolvulus cneorum subsp. latifolius is a subspecies of the Silverbush or Wolf's Milk plant, characterized by its broad leaves and silvery-gray foliage. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its alkaloid and flavonoid content. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic agent. Convolvulus cneorum subsp. latifolius can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
